props.message:
THE TOOLSHED

A wall of twenty-seven tools. Each handle smoothed by hands that worked. Hammers, chisels, looms, kettles, knives, files, awls, rasps, gimlets, planes, mallets, drawknives, augers, gouges, burins, scribes, dividers, calipers, shears, snips, tongs, pincers, pliers, prybars, pickers, pokers, prods.

Twenty-seven things that work.

Tool wall, west run.

Worn handles. Bright edges. Each hung in its own peg. The Wyrdkin who set this wall said: 'A tool that lives on a peg is a tool that knows where to go home.'

Tool wall, east run.

The smaller things — awls, scribes, gimlets. The kind of tool you reach for when you've already tried the bigger one and a quieter answer is needed.

root.speaker: Old Toolwright Marn
root.text:
Well met, walker. Mind the floor — there's a chisel somewhere I haven't found yet.

You after a tool, or after a story?

tools.speaker: Old Toolwright Marn
tools.text:
Twenty-seven on the wall. Each one shaped for a thing the realm needs every day.

A hammer is a hammer. A scribe is a scribe. There's no nobler tool than the one you can name without thinking.

unnamed.speaker: Old Toolwright Marn
unnamed.text:
Then it's not a tool yet. It's a thing that happens to be in your hand.

By the Source — name it and it'll start working.

appr.speaker: Old Toolwright Marn
appr.text:
Pell thinks the third hammer from the left is the canonical one. I think it's the seventh. We've been at this since spring.

The truth is they're both right. There's no canonical hammer. But don't tell Pell.

story.speaker: Old Toolwright Marn
story.text:
My grandmother strung this wall. She said the Wyrdmark would never be lost so long as someone could pick a tool off it and remember what to do.

So far, so good.

bye.speaker: Old Toolwright Marn
bye.text: Walk well. And mind the chisel.

root.speaker: Apprentice Pell
root.text: You! Settle this. Third hammer from the left, or seventh? Marn says seventh. Marn is wrong.

third.speaker: Apprentice Pell
third.text: Thank you. Finally. A walker with eyes.

seventh.speaker: Apprentice Pell
seventh.text:
Hmph. Marn's got to you.

Look again next time, all right? Third. It's third.

fine.speaker: Apprentice Pell
fine.text: Don't say that. Don't ever say that here.

bye.speaker: Apprentice Pell
bye.text: Walk well. Don't trust Marn on hammers.

props.message:
Stone tablet, weather-smoothed:

    echo "Hello, world!"

    Hello, world!

The first program ever written by anyone in The Mount.
We have not improved on it.

Posted next to the oldest tool-rack:

    1993.
    Oldest still-walking among us.

*Beneath, in newer hand:* she does not require the newer tools. she does not provide them either.
